mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in RSAFS signals strengthening household demand and upward revenue revisions for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the rolling 3-year walk-forward Rank IC between 3-month RSAFS growth and 20-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03 or negative in more than half of evaluated windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.5%, the policy stance signals emergency liquidity support and lower discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have mean 60-day S&P 500 returns no higher than untriggered observations, or if positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth combined with a falling UNRATE signals broad labor-market repair, improving income expectations and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down trigger does not outperform either single-series signal and produces nonpositive average 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO year-over-year growth crosses above zero, the economy exits an industrial contraction, lifting operating leverage and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO recoveries are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 50% of cases or have average returns below the full-sample average.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL acceleration while FEDFUNDS is not falling raises expected real policy restraint and margin pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above its 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS 3-month change >= 0
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction trigger has average 20-day S&P 500 returns above zero or fails to underperform CPI acceleration alone by at least 25 basis points per event.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST growth crosses sharply upward, housing demand leads construction activity, durable-goods demand, and credit sentiment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST trigger events have a 60-day S&P 500 hit rate below 50% or if their mean return is not statistically above nontriggered periods at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating household income and recession risk, forcing equity investors to cut earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change crosses above 0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE deterioration is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of events or has a nonnegative average forward return.

mechanism_narrative: GDPC1 growth crossing from contraction to expansion confirms aggregate demand stabilization and reduces recession tail risk, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses from below 0% to above 0%
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth-crossing events do not produce higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative-growth quarters or if the walk-forward hit rate is below 50%.
